title: Native, Hybrid & Web App Testing
overview: Differentiate and test Native (mobile locators), Hybrid (WebView context switching), and Web (browser app) applications using Appium.
whyItMatters: An SDET must know which context they are in to choose the right locators. Selenium locators won't work in a native app, and Mobile locators won't work inside a WebView.
explanation: Native apps use platform UI components (Swift/Kotlin). Hybrid apps are native shells embedding a web browser (WebView) using frameworks like Ionic/React Native. Mobile Web apps run in Chrome/Safari on the phone. For Hybrid apps, Appium starts in a `NATIVE_APP` context. You must get available contexts (`getContextHandles()`), find the `WEBVIEW` context, and switch to it (`context()`). Once in the WEBVIEW, you use standard Selenium locators (CSS/XPath) and DOM interactions.
keyPoints: Native Apps: Use Accessibility ID and native locators.
Hybrid Apps: Switch context to `WEBVIEW_<id>` to interact with HTML elements.
Mobile Web: Set `browserName` capability (e.g., Chrome, Safari) instead of `app`.
ChromeDriver must match the WebView/Chrome version on the Android device.
realWorldExample: In a banking app, the login screen is Native, but the 'Terms and Conditions' is a WebView. The script interacts with Native locators to log in, switches to WEBVIEW context to scroll and click 'Agree', then switches back to NATIVE_APP.
commonMistakes: Trying to click an HTML element inside a WebView without switching contexts.
Not having the correct version of ChromeDriver installed on the Appium server for the device's Android WebView version.
bestPractices: Use `chromedriverExecutable` capability if you face version mismatch errors in Hybrid Android apps.
Always switch back to `NATIVE_APP` context immediately after completing WebView interactions to avoid stale references.

interviewQuestions.q: How do you automate a Hybrid application in Appium?
interviewQuestions.a: You must retrieve the available contexts using getContextHandles(), switch the driver context from NATIVE_APP to the WEBVIEW context, perform the interactions using web locators, and then switch back to NATIVE_APP.

revisionSummary: Appium handles Native, Hybrid, and Mobile Web apps. Hybrid testing requires switching the driver context to `WEBVIEW` to interact with the embedded HTML DOM using standard Selenium locators.
